title: Backstepping Boundary Stabilization of a Class of Coupled Parabolic PDEs with Spatially Varying Coefficients
authors: P. Biazetto, C. Laurent, A. Ferreira
abstract: We design a boundary feedback law that exponentially stabilizes a system of coupled reaction–diffusion equations with spatially varying reaction terms. The control kernel is obtained from a well-posed Goursat problem, and exponential stability is proved in the L2 norm using a strict Lyapunov functional. Numerical experiments confirm the predicted decay rates.

title: Practical Exponential Stability of a Reduced-Order Thermoacoustic Model via Dynamic Mode Decomposition with Control
abstract: A data-driven reduced-order model of a Rijke-tube thermoacoustic system is constructed using dynamic mode decomposition with control. We establish practical exponential stability of the closed loop on a finite-dimensional product space, making explicit the analytical bridges required to transfer the guarantee back to the underlying infinite-dimensional dynamics.

title: Distributionally Robust Constraint-Tightening Model Predictive Control for Freeway Traffic Flow
authors: P. Biazetto, S. Bianchi, T. Nakamura
venue: SIAM Journal on Control and Optimization
abstract: Building on the Lighthill–Whitham–Richards macroscopic model, we formulate a model predictive controller that tightens constraints using a Wasserstein ambiguity set and a conditional value-at-risk reformulation. A moving-boundary reparameterization in the emission domain yields a tractable quadratic program solved online with OSQP.

title: Resolvent Estimates and Well-Posedness for Boundary-Controlled Hyperbolic Systems on Networks
venue: ESAIM: Control, Optimisation and Calculus of Variations
abstract: We characterize the resolvent set of the generator associated with a boundary-controlled hyperbolic system posed on a metric graph and prove generation of a strongly continuous semigroup. The estimates yield input-to-state stability with respect to boundary disturbances.

title: Separation Principle for Observer-Based Boundary Control of Infinite-Dimensional Systems
venue: IEEE Conference on Decision and Control (CDC)
abstract: We establish a separation principle for a class of boundary observer–controller pairs acting on abstract evolution equations, giving sufficient conditions under which the cascade of a stable observer and a stabilizing feedback preserves closed-loop exponential stability.

title: Learning Control Kernels for Reaction–Diffusion Systems with Neural Operators
authors: P. Biazetto, L. Park, R. Okafor
abstract: We approximate backstepping control kernels with a neural operator, reducing online computation by two orders of magnitude while retaining provable stability margins on a benchmark family of parabolic systems.

title: Robust Output Regulation of Boundary-Controlled Wave Equations under Parametric Uncertainty
authors: P. Biazetto, H. Vogel, S. Bianchi
abstract: An internal-model-based regulator is designed for a wave equation with uncertain wave speed and boundary actuation, achieving asymptotic tracking of polynomial and sinusoidal references despite bounded parametric perturbations.

title: Lyapunov Methods for Infinite-Dimensional Systems: A Practitioner's Guide
abstract: This chapter surveys strict and non-strict Lyapunov functionals for partial differential equations, with worked examples spanning parabolic, hyperbolic, and coupled systems, and a discussion of common pitfalls when extending finite-dimensional intuition to Hilbert spaces.

title: Adaptive Boundary Control of Parabolic PDEs with Unknown Diffusivity
abstract: We propose an adaptive boundary controller that simultaneously estimates an unknown diffusion coefficient and stabilizes the plant, with a Lyapunov argument that couples parameter estimation error and state energy.

title: Constraint-Tightening MPC for Conservation Laws with Moving Bottlenecks
abstract: We extend constraint-tightening predictive control to scalar conservation laws coupled with a moving interior bottleneck, deriving the shock-velocity sensitivity terms needed for a consistent linearization of the optimal control problem.

title: Input-to-State Stability of Semilinear Parabolic Equations with Boundary Disturbances
authors: P. Biazetto, R. Okafor, H. Vogel
abstract: Sufficient conditions are given for input-to-state stability of semilinear parabolic equations subject to disturbances entering through the boundary, using monotonicity arguments and a De Giorgi iteration.

title: Real-Time Implementation of Backstepping Controllers on Embedded Hardware
abstract: We report a fixed-point embedded implementation of a backstepping boundary controller for a heat-exchanger testbed, analyzing the trade-off between kernel discretization resolution and closed-loop performance.
